summary: Kenneth demonstrates a real-money covered call selling strategy across three stocks (D-Wave, Archer Aviation, HIMS) targeting weekly premiums. He generated $633 in net premium income on a $14,000 deployed capital base (4.5% weekly return) while managing concurrent cash-secured put obligations and maintaining vacation flexibility.
takeaways: Sell covered calls expiring Friday for weekly premium collection
Target 4-5% weekly premium yields on covered call positions
Deploy $5,000 per position for consistent weekly income generation
Maintain cash reserves equal to potential assignment obligations (e.g., $15,000 for three CSP contracts at $5,000 each)
Execution time: ~20 minutes research + 30 minutes trading work per week
Premium percentages achieved: D-Wave 4.6%, Archer Aviation 5.3%, HIMS 4%
Role premium as income (tax view) even if rolling reduces net profit temporarily

strategy_candidates.change: Systematize position sizing rule: allocate $5,000 per covered call to generate $200–235 weekly premium per position, scaling to $1,250/mo target by adding positions proportionally
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth deployed three $5,000 positions in one session totaling $647 premium (4.5%), demonstrating replicable unit economics. At $200–235/position/week, Brian would need 5–6 concurrent positions to reach $1,250/mo target

title: Part 11 - 8/11/25: Can I make $180 PER WEEK From HIMS Covered Calls?
